track_description: Very small background tuning and insulation improvement on incumbent high-temperature fossil heat systems.
evidence_summary: Australian industrial-heat evidence supports only a very small background efficiency drift at the current high-temperature role boundary because route change dominates the larger decarbonisation story.
derivation_method: Expose a deliberately small background drift by rebaselining the fossil incumbent row upward and applying the explicit autonomous track so the modeled pathway remains stable under the baseline control setting.
double_counting_guardrail: Fossil incumbent only. Do not apply to electrified or low-carbon-fuel methods, and keep the explicit drift small enough that it cannot be mistaken for a route-change wedge.
review_notes: This is intentionally tiny and fossil-only so it cannot be mistaken for a broader high-temperature route-change wedge.
